title: ai-usage
tagline_grad: AI spend, quota, and token visibility.
lead: A local workstation CLI that reports balance, period spend, subscription quota, token usage, and history across AI providers without hiding partial failures.
sub: It loads credentials from local files, queries provider APIs or local developer-tool state, normalizes everything into one model, saves snapshots, and renders table or JSON output.
features_intro: The tool is narrow on purpose: one normalized reporting surface over heterogeneous billing, quota, and token-usage providers.

features.title: Balance and spend
features.body: DeepSeek, xAI, OpenRouter, Vast.ai, Exa, X API, and Nous feed the API-credit side of the report when credentials are present.

features.title: Subscription quotas
features.body: Codex, Claude Code, and Google AI Studio render session, weekly, or per-model quota windows separately from API-credit balances.

features.title: Token accounting
features.body: Providers that expose usage return cached input, uncached input, output, totals, and optional per-model rows.

features.title: Table or JSON
features.body: The same normalized data can be read as aligned terminal tables or machine-readable JSON with api and subscription branches.

features.title: Local history
features.body: Every live fetch saves normalized snapshot rows in a local SQLite database for --history reports and provider-specific filtering.

features.title: Visible failures
features.body: Missing auth, intentional skips, timeouts, and provider-specific errors stay visible through ProviderData.meta instead of erasing the row.

flow_intro: The runtime spine is small and auditable: parse, load, fetch, normalize, persist, render.

flow.title: Parse operator intent
flow.body: argparse resolves provider selection, model detail, JSON output, history mode, and the Nous refresh helper.

flow.title: Load local credentials
flow.body: config.load_credentials reads ~/.hermes/.env, fallback key files, Hermes auth state, and provider-specific OAuth files.

flow.title: Build provider adapters
flow.body: The registry constructs only the requested Provider subclasses, preserving the registered provider order for output.

flow.title: Fetch with isolation
flow.body: fetch_all runs provider.fetch() calls concurrently when useful, backed by a retrying urllib HTTP client and total timeout.

flow.title: Normalize provider data
flow.body: Provider modules convert raw HTTP, CLI, or local-file responses into ProviderData, TokenData, extras, and metadata.

flow.title: Save snapshot rows
flow.body: SnapshotDB writes normalized balance, spend, and token columns to ~/.hermes/ai-usage.db; Codex accounts get account-qualified keys.

flow.title: Render the report
flow.body: render_table, render_json, render_history, or render_history_json produces the operator-facing output.

model_intro: Think of ai-usage as a narrow reporting hub: provider-specific adapters on the right, one normalized local report on the left.
model_cap: Each provider keeps its quirks inside its adapter; the CLI, renderer, and history database consume one normalized shape.
commands_intro: The CLI surface is intentionally compact: provider selection, model detail, JSON, history, and one auth-refresh helper.

commands.touches: All registered providers, local credentials, snapshot DB, table renderer
Per-model rows when provider responses include model detail
Nous OAuth state in Hermes auth data

audiences_intro: Three doors in, depending on whether you need value, operation, or implementation depth.

audiences.title: Executive Brief
audiences.body: Value, maturity signals, and risk posture in a short code-free read.

audiences.title: User Guide
audiences.body: First-run journey, command routing, output rules, and troubleshooting.

audiences.title: Architecture & C4
audiences.body: Source-level architecture, normalized data model, generated C4 atlas, and ADRs.

boundary_intro: The repo documents credential locations and normalized fields, but real credentials and raw provider payloads stay outside committed artifacts.
boundary_cap: The dashed paths are documentation rules: credentials are read locally, normalized snapshots are stored locally, and raw provider payloads are not the committed source of truth.
boundary_note: Data-boundary details are maintained in Data Architecture; credential handling rules are in User Guide and README.
docmap_intro: This page is the visual front door. Markdown stays canonical; rendered HTML companions keep browser navigation styled and drift-checked.

docmap.owns: Reading path and generated artifact contract

footer_provenance: Metrics grounded on 2026-06-28: 112 tests / 17 files / 18 suites from scripts/generate_test_inventory.py and pytest collection; 10 providers from src/ai_usage/providers; 5 C4 views and 3 ADRs from docs/architecture. No credential values or provider payloads are embedded.
